verdict_reasoning: TGOLN2 (TGN46) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein with steady-state TGN localization but well-documented constitutive cycling to the plasma membrane via CARTS (TGN-to-PM carriers), followed by retrieval via early endosomes driven by a cytosolic YQRL tyrosine-based endocytic motif. The lumenal domain faces the extracellular space at the PM. Numerous studies confirm a minority but real PM pool; the dominant compartment is the TGN/endosomal system. This fits dual_localization: the protein has its own TM anchor and reaches the outer face of the PM during constitutive vesicular cycling, even though the TGN is the steady-state home.
